title: Horoměřice

infobox.key: [Authority control databases](/wiki/Help:Authority_control "Help:Authority control"): National [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q2625864#identifiers "Edit this at Wikidata")

infobox.value: is a municipality and village in [Prague-West District](/wiki/Prague-West_District "Prague-West District") in the [Central Bohemian Region](/wiki/Central_Bohemian_Region "Central Bohemian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 5,500 inhabitants making it the [most populous municipality in the country without the town status](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_the_Czech_Republic#Most_populated_municipalities_without_the_town_status "List of cities and towns in the Czech Republic").

["Population of Municipalities – 1 January 2025"](https://csu.gov.cz/produkty/population-of-municipalities-t4l3n8d2iw). [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2025-05-16.

["Historický lexikon obcí České republiky 1869–2011"](https://csu.gov.cz/produkty/historicky-lexikon-obci-1869-az-2015) (in Czech). [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2015-12-21.

**Horoměřice** is a municipality and village in [Prague-West District](/wiki/Prague-West_District "Prague-West District") in the [Central Bohemian Region](/wiki/Central_Bohemian_Region "Central Bohemian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 5,500 inhabitants making it the [most populous municipality in the country without the town status](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_the_Czech_Republic#Most_populated_municipalities_without_the_town_status "List of cities and towns in the Czech Republic").

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Horom%C4%9B%C5%99ice&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Horoměřice is situated northwest of [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague"), in its immediate vicinity. It lies in an agricultural landscape of the [Prague Plateau](/wiki/Prague_Plateau "Prague Plateau").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Horom%C4%9B%C5%99ice&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The first written mention of Horoměřice is from 1273. Back then it was owned by the [Strahov Monastery](/wiki/Strahov_Monastery "Strahov Monastery"). The monastery lost the village during the [Hussite Wars](/wiki/Hussite_Wars "Hussite Wars") in 1421, but after changing hands several times, the monastery owned it continuously from 1598 until establishment of the municipality.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Horom%C4%9B%C5%99ice&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Transport")]

There are no railways or major roads passing through the municipality.

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Horom%C4%9B%C5%99ice&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sights")]

In the municipality is a simple castle from the 18th century, which belonged to the Strahov Monastery until 1945. Today it is owned by the municipality, which intends to reconstruct and use it for social and cultural purposes.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Horom%C4%9B%C5%99ice&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Benedikt Roezl](/wiki/Benedikt_Roezl "Benedikt Roezl") (1824–1885), traveller, gardener and botanist
